sections.paragraph:
ἁρμογή, ῆς, ἡ (s. ἁρμόζω; Polyb. et al.; POslo 13 II, 14f grammatical t.t.; Mel., P. 55, 405) joint in masonry, where one stone touches the others (Jos., Ant. 15, 399) Hv 3, 2, 6; 3, 5, 1f; Hs 9, 9, 7.—DELG s.v. ἅρμα.

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

POslo POslo = Papyri Osloenses—List 4

t.t. t.t. = terminus technicus (termini technici), technical term(s)

Mel. Mel. = Melito of Sardis, II a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Hv Hv = Visions

Hs Hs = Similitudes

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 132
